laws.title: Public Law 12-81 | The Integrity Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates a general anti-withdrawal rule and repeal-conflict language, but it does not repeal itself or sunset. Section 5 is still operative: 'The President shall not, without Senate consultation and approval, remove the United States from any treaty or international organization...'.

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Integrity Act."
provisions.notes: Keep only a concise short-title note.

provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable ... and shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede this Act's provisions.
provisions.notes: Editorial savings note only.

provisions.evidence: Any part of any act, joint resolution, or codified law that conflicts with this Act shall be considered null and void.
provisions.notes: No specific Code target is stated; do not invent one.

provisions.text_summary: Findings about treaties and international organizations.
provisions.evidence: The Congress finds that the President should not be able to withdraw the United States from treaties or international organizations without Senate consultation.
provisions.notes: Findings only.

provisions.text_summary: Prohibits presidential withdrawal from treaties or international organizations without Senate process.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new foreign-relations provision barring withdrawal from treaties or international organizations unless the President consults the Senate, a senator petitions for withdrawal, and two-thirds of the Senate approves a resolution.
provisions.evidence: The President shall not, without Senate consultation and approval, remove the United States from any treaty or international organization ... two-thirds of the Senate ... approve a resolution ...
provisions.notes: Substantive general rule is missing from the current XML and should be codified in Title 22 rather than left as a note.

current_implementation.detail: The only current treatment is a boilerplate statutory note at Title 28 section 1 with a full quoted-content dump and Trello link. The substantive anti-withdrawal rule from SEC 5 is not actually codified.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012081-codification.
Codify SEC 5 as a real Title 22 provision; keep SEC 1-4 only as concise note material.

laws.title: Public Law 12-82 | Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act 2023
laws.status_basis: The source directly amends Title 10 and does not self-repeal. The operative amendments are explicit, including 'Section 531 of title 10, United States Code, is amended' and the revised Joint Chiefs language in section 151.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and definitions/effective-date scaffolding.
provisions.evidence: Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act 2023 ... This Act shall be severable ... This Act shall take effect immediately.
provisions.notes: Note material only.

provisions.exact_change: Replace the original-appointments text in section 531 with the amended commissioning rules enacted by SEC 4.
provisions.evidence: "Section 531 of title 10, United States Code, is amended ..."
provisions.notes: This is a required textual amendment, not a note.

provisions.evidence: section 12204 ... is repealed.
provisions.notes: Revisions should be reflected as repeal marking.

provisions.exact_change: Replace the affected text in section 12203 with the amended appointment rules.
provisions.evidence: section 12203 ... is amended ...
provisions.notes: Direct textual amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Promotion-discretion, conflict, and administrative-policy provisions with no discrete Code target.
provisions.evidence: promotion-discretion / blanket conflict / administrative policy language
provisions.notes: Keep only as concise administrative note material.

provisions.exact_change: Replace the Joint Chiefs section with the amended composition and functions text, including the new subsection (g).
provisions.evidence: "section 151 of title 10, United States Code, is amended ..."
provisions.notes: This amendment is already visible in the current XML.

provisions.evidence: effective date / applicability language
provisions.notes: No separate Code section required.

current_implementation.detail: The XML contains the amended Title 10 text for sections 531 and 151, but it still carries a full-text codification dump and an extra USAR note. The repealed section 12204 should be verified as a true repeal marking, and the boilerplate note layer still needs cleanup.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012082-codification.
Keep the Title 10 textual amendments for 531, 12203, 12204, and 151.
Delete the extra rp-pl012082-151-note if it duplicates the incorporated amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 12-83 | Dannybec Went to Jail Act
laws.status_basis: The source is an enactment that repeals prior Public Law 7-42 and shuts down the Department of Transportation, but it does not repeal itself. The act's own text is operative on enactment.

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Dannybec Went to Jail Act."
It is the sense of Congress that ...
provisions.notes: Sense language only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 7-42 and shuts down the Department of Transportation.
provisions.exact_change: Record the repeal of Public Law 7-42 as a cross-reference only; do not create permanent U.S. Code text.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 7-42 ... shall be repealed, and the Department of Transportation shall be shut down.
provisions.notes: The repealed-law cross-reference is the only codification-relevant piece.

provisions.evidence: This act shall take effect immediately.

current_implementation.detail: The current note treats this as statutory history, but the source is a current enactment. The note also includes a full quoted-content dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012083-codification.
Replace the current boilerplate with a concise note and a repeal cross-reference for Public Law 7-42.

laws.title: Public Law 12-84 | Municipal Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: This is a current local/District municipal enactment that reinstates the City Charter and reorganizes local government. The source does not self-repeal; it is not federal positive-law text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purposes, and definitions for the municipal act.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Municipal Act of 2023."
provisions.notes: No U.S. Code placement.

provisions.text_summary: Municipal reorganization, repeal/reinstatement, and charter amendments.
provisions.exact_change: Treat as local/District charter material; do not push into permanent U.S. Code text.
provisions.evidence: The District of Columbia City Charter is once again reinstated in its entirety.
provisions.notes: Local law only.

provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable ... will be in effect immediately upon full passage.
provisions.notes: Not U.S. Code text.

current_implementation.detail: A boilerplate note with a full-text dump and Trello link is sitting in Title 40, but the law is local/District governance and should not be in the permanent U.S. Code.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012084-codification.
Exclude the act from permanent U.S. Code text; keep at most a short local-history note.

laws.title: Public Law 12-85 | The Congress Strikes Back Act
laws.status_basis: The source is an individual disqualification/bill-of-attainder-style enactment against a named person. It is current as enacted and does not self-repeal, but it is not suitable for permanent positive-law codification.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall be referred to as the "The Congress Strikes Back Act."
provisions.notes: Introductory and timing provisions only.

provisions.text_summary: Declares hudson88888 an enemy of the United States and bars office-holding.
provisions.exact_change: Do not codify this private/individual disqualification in permanent U.S. Code text.
provisions.evidence: hudson88888 will be declared an enemy of the United States ... disqualified to hold and enjoy any office of honor, trust, or profit under the United States.
provisions.notes: Individual-specific measure only.

current_implementation.detail: The note is placed in the Congress title with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the underlying enactment is an individual/private barment and should not be in permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012085-codification.
Exclude from permanent Code; keep at most a one-line historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 12-86 | H.R. 1302
laws.status_basis: The source is a current local/District repeal of Title II, section 7 of the fictional New District of Columbia Home Rule Act. It is not self-repealing and does not change federal U.S. Code text.

provisions.evidence: This bill may be cited as "H.R. 1302".

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Title II, section 7 of the New District of Columbia Home Rule Act.
provisions.exact_change: Record the repeal as a local/District cross-reference; do not insert U.S. Code text.
provisions.evidence: "TITLE II, SEC. 007" ... "shall be repealed"
provisions.notes: Applies to the fictional D.C. home-rule act, not to the federal Code.

current_implementation.detail: The note is parked in Title 40 with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the source only repeals a local/District charter provision.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012086-codification.
Exclude from permanent U.S. Code text and keep only a concise local repeal note tied to the D.C. home-rule act.

laws.title: Public Law 12-87 | An act to repeal the Smart Label Congress Act (Public Law 9-54)
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 9-54 and directs each chamber to handle legislative labeling and archivist filing. That repeal is operative on enactment; the act itself is not self-repealed.

provisions.evidence: This bill may be cited by its long title as "An act to repeal the Smart Label Congress Act (Public Law 9-54)"
This Act shall be severable ... This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless ...
provisions.notes: Note material only.

provisions.exact_change: Add a repeal cross-reference for Public Law 9-54.
provisions.evidence: the Smart Label Congress Act (Public Law 9-54) shall immediately be null and void
provisions.notes: The repealed law should be marked historically.

provisions.evidence: it shall be the duty of each respective chamber ... to determine their labelling system ... and file ... to the National Archivist
provisions.notes: Internal congressional administration, not permanent Code text.

provisions.evidence: This Act shall take effect immediately after its ratification

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the Congress title, but it still has a full quoted-content dump and Trello link. The repeal of PL 9-54 is not separately marked as a historical cross-reference.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012087-codification.
Add a repeal cross-reference for PL 9-54 and keep the chamber-labeling rule only as a concise note.

laws.title: Public Law 12-88 | New District of Columbia Home Rule Act

provisions.evidence: This Act may be referred to as the "New District of Columbia Home Rule Act."
provisions.notes: The real Pub. L. 93-198 citation is referenced only within the act's fiction.

provisions.text_summary: Current officers and oath requirements; section 7 is later repealed by PL 12-86.

provisions.evidence: All current officers, elected and appointed, shall restate the oath under this Act within 5 days
provisions.notes: Local/District only.

provisions.text_summary: District Charter structure: Council, Mayor, Judiciary venue, Attorney General, and related powers.

provisions.evidence: the Chief of the Metropolitan Police shall assist the Secret Service ... under section 302 to title 3 and section 3056 of title 18
provisions.notes: The title 3 and title 18 citations are references only.

provisions.text_summary: Congressional authority reservation, militia carve-out, initiatives, recalls, agreements, and oath.
provisions.evidence: the Congress of the United States reserves the right ... to exercise its constitutional authority as legislature for the District

Exclude from permanent U.S. Code text and keep only a short historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 12-103 | Ceremonial 5-Star Officer Creation Act
laws.status_basis: The source establishes a single O-11 / five-star officer framework and does not self-repeal. It is a substantive military-grade rule, not a mere ceremonial note.

provisions.evidence: This act may be referred to as the "Ceremonial 5-Star Officer Creation Act"

provisions.text_summary: Creates the O-11 / five-star officer rule, duties, appointment conditions, tenure, and retirement/re-entry rules.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Title 10 provision creating the O-11 grade for a single five-star officer and setting the appointment, service, and retirement rules.
provisions.evidence: The equivalent ranks of General of the Army, General of the Air Force, and Fleet Admiral ... shall be a commissioned officer with the grade of O-11.
provisions.notes: This belongs in Title 10, not Title 36.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effectiveness, severability, and conflict language.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage ...
provisions.notes: Note material only.

current_implementation.detail: The current note sits in Title 36 (patriotic/ceremonial material) with a full-text dump and Trello link, but the source creates a substantive military pay-grade rule that should be codified in Title 10.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl012103-codification.
Move the substantive O-11 provision to Title 10 as a real code section; keep only short introductory note material.

laws.title: Public Law 14-89 | Americans Safety and Smart Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 2-6 and creates new criminal offenses for law-enforcement misconduct and public-way obstruction. It is current, not self-repealed, and its substantive offenses are not yet reflected as real text in the XML.

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Americans Safety and Smart Act of 2023."

provisions.exact_change: Add a repeal cross-reference for Public Law 2-6.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 2-6 | Shelby-Kabob Act of 2021 shall be repealed entirely.
provisions.notes: Historical repeal only.

provisions.text_summary: Creates color-of-law misconduct offenses for law-enforcement officers.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Title 18 criminal offense covering fraudulent detention/arrest, warrantless seizure, and excessive-force conduct by officers acting under color of law.
provisions.evidence: It shall be unlawful for any law enforcement officer acting under the color of law to fraudulently detain or arrest any individual ...
provisions.notes: The source's self-placement in 'Title 1' is anomalous; Title 18 is the substantive home.

provisions.text_summary: Creates public-way barricade and interference offenses.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Title 18 offense for barricading public ways to obstruct peaceful protest and for interfering with law enforcement.
provisions.evidence: It shall be unlawful for any person to attempt to barricade, block, or attempt to suspend the use of any sidewalk or public roadway accessible to the public ...
provisions.notes: Also should be codified in Title 18 rather than left as a note.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effect and self-designated placement instructions.
provisions.evidence: This act shall immediately take effect upon passage and signature.
provisions.notes: The source's Chapter 1000 of Title 1 instruction is editorially anomalous.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML has only a short note and Trello link in Title 18; the substantive criminal provisions were not actually created. The PL 2-6 repeal is also not separately marked.

recommended_actions: Remove the Trello link from rp-pl014089-codification.
Codify SEC 4-5 as real Title 18 offense sections; do not use the source's anomalous Title 1 placement.
Add a repeal cross-reference for PL 2-6.
